signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD 1W outperformance (+1.097%) and NZD weakness (-0.792%) create a 1.889% G10 spread that favors further downside toward 0.5700. Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz triggers a safe-haven bid for the USD, while NZD COT net short -22.3k and a -0.83% real rate disadvantage reinforce the laggard profile. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 July is the catalyst for assessing if the hike bias can offset this structural weakness.
evidence: Iran closing Strait of Hormuz headline

signals.title: EUR/USD — Hawkish Fed Repricing + ECB Divergence
signals.text: EUR COT net short -49.2k (37.4% long) aligns with the 150bp carry disadvantage to keep pressure on the 1.1469 Friday close. Hawkish Fed debut by Warsh and a restrictive +1.48% US real rate contrast with the ECB's neutral data surprises (CESI +0), driving the pair toward the 1.1400 handle. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 July determines if the current hiking cycle can sustain any recovery against USD dominance.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Advantage vs Squeeze Risk
signals.text: JPY COT net short -115k creates significant short-squeeze risk despite the 275bp USD carry advantage and the pair's Friday close at 161.28. While the BoJ maintains a hike bias and JPY shows a strong positive CESI (+17), the 2.90% US-JGB yield gap continues to incentivize carry trades until a volatility spike occurs. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 31 July is the catalyst for a shift in the yield regime.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Sentiment Extreme + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD retail sentiment at 95% short signals a contrarian continuation risk as the pair decouples from WTI oil (z = -1.94σ). CAD COT net short -60.6k and a 150bp carry disadvantage against the USD support the 1W trend (+1.29%), even as oil prices remain elevated due to Middle East tensions. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 July serves as the catalyst to resolve the current divergence from energy prices.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind + Data Misses
signals.text: AUD COT net long +38.1k (68.8% long) faces liquidation risk as the pair closed Friday at 0.7016 amid a negative economic surprise index (CESI -8). Although the AUD maintains a 60bp carry advantage over the USD, the hawkish Fed repricing and 15.0% IV (VRP +7.2%) suggest hedging demand is rising against the Aussie. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 August is the catalyst for confirming the sustainability of the current yield advantage.
